privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.noticeBody: This policy applies to the use of InvoData. Using the service implies acceptance of this policy.
privacy.section1Body: Welcome to InvoData, a platform for invoice management and analysis. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and protect your information when you use our service.
privacy.section2Body: We collect the following types of information:
privacy.section2Item1: Account information: name, email, username, language and preferences (and VAT ID if provided).
privacy.section2Item2: Invoices and documents: document number, dates, amounts, payment method, issuer VAT ID, items and notes.
privacy.section2Item3: Uploads and files: uploaded files and related metadata.
privacy.section2Item4: Chats (optional): messages and summaries when the chat module is enabled.
privacy.section2Item6: Usage data: service interactions, used features, and relevant audit records.
privacy.section3Body: We use the collected information to:
privacy.section3Item1: Provide, maintain and improve the service.
privacy.section3Item3: Process invoices and generate reports and dashboards.
privacy.section3Item4: Detect, prevent and resolve technical and security issues.
privacy.section4Body: We do not sell your personal data. We may share your information in the following circumstances:
privacy.section4Item1: Service providers: cloud infrastructure, storage and backups required to operate the service.
privacy.section4Item2: Legal compliance: when required by law or a competent authority.
privacy.section4Item3: Protection of rights: to protect the security, integrity and rights of the service and users.
privacy.section5Body: We implement technical and organizational measures to protect your information. Data is encrypted in transit (TLS) and at rest with user-derived keys that are not stored on the server. Still, no transmission or storage method is 100% secure.
privacy.section7Body: Depending on your location, you may have the following rights regarding your personal data:
privacy.section7Process: To exercise these rights, contact us at {{email}}.
privacy.section9Body: We may update this Privacy Policy periodically. The last updated date will be shown at the top.
privacy.section10Body: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, contact us at: {{email}}

terms.title: Terms and Conditions
terms.section1Body: By accessing or using InvoData, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Use. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, you may not access the service.
terms.section2Body: InvoData is a platform for invoice management and analysis with upload, processing, dashboards, and (when enabled) AI assistance. The service is provided "as is" and may be subject to changes or interruptions without notice.
terms.section3Body: To access certain features, you may need to create an account. You are responsible for keeping your credentials confidential and for all activity on your account. You must notify us immediately of any unauthorized use.
terms.section4Body: By using our service, you agree not to:
terms.section4Item1: Use the service for any unlawful purpose or in violation of these terms.
terms.section4Item2: Violate applicable laws, including intellectual property laws.
terms.section4Item3: Submit unlawful, offensive, threatening, defamatory, or privacy-violating content.
terms.section4Item5: Interfere with the proper operation of the service.
terms.section4Item6: Collect or store personal data of other users without consent.
terms.section5Body: The service and its original content, features, and design are and will remain the exclusive property of InvoData and its licensors. Trademarks and trade dress may not be used without prior written permission.
terms.section6Body: By submitting content, you grant us a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use, reproduce, and process that content only to the extent necessary to operate the service. You retain all rights to the content you submit, post, or display.
terms.section6Body2: We respect your privacy and process personal data under the GDPR. For details, see our Privacy Policy.
terms.section7Body: Under the GDPR, you have the right to:
terms.section7Item1: Access your personal data we process.
terms.section7Item3: Request deletion of your data in certain circumstances.
terms.section7Item6: Withdraw consent when processing is based on consent.
terms.section7Contact: To exercise these rights, contact us at {{email}}.
terms.section8Body: InvoData uses AI to process documents and, when enabled, to respond to chat interactions. By using our service:
terms.section8Item1: You acknowledge that AI-generated responses may not be perfect.
terms.section8Item2: You understand we may use interactions to improve the service in line with the Privacy Policy.
terms.section8Item3: You are aware that, in some circumstances, interactions may be reviewed by humans for quality control.
terms.section9Body: Under no circumstances shall InvoData, its directors, employees, partners, agents, suppliers, or affiliates be liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, data, or use.
terms.section10Body: The service is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind. We do not guarantee uninterrupted, timely, secure, or error-free service.
terms.section11Body: You agree to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless InvoData and its licensors, service providers, employees, agents, directors, and representatives from any claims, liabilities, damages, losses, costs, or expenses arising from your violation of these Terms.
terms.section12Body: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal data. Data is encrypted in transit and at rest with user-derived keys that are not stored on the server. No method of transmission or storage is 100% secure.
terms.section13Body: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. If a revision is material, we will try to provide at least 30 days' notice.
terms.section14Body: These terms are governed by the laws of Portugal.
terms.section15Body: Any dispute will be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Lisbon, Portugal, without prejudice to your right to lodge a complaint with the competent data protection authority.
terms.section16Body: If any provision of these Terms is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will remain in full force and effect.
terms.section17Body: If you have questions about these Terms of Use, contact us at: {{email}}
terms.section18Body: You have the right to lodge a complaint with the Portuguese Data Protection Authority (CNPD) if you believe your personal data is processed in violation of applicable law.

share.title: Share invoice
share.description: Create a link or share with another user. Configure PDF view/download access below.

share.allowPdfHint: Let the recipient view the PDF.
share.allowPdfDownloadHint: Let the recipient download the PDF file.
share.pdfEncryptionHint: The PDF is unavailable because storage encryption is enabled.
share.publicHint: Anyone with the link can view the invoice.
share.userSharedNotice: Share created. The user has been notified.
share.loginRequired: Sign in to access this share.
